_comment: Yashigani SANDBOXED-EXTRACTOR seccomp profile (plan §6 B1, Captain; Su security-reviewed + hardened 2026-06-09). defaultAction SCMP_ACT_ERRNO => every syscall NOT listed is blocked (fail-closed). This jail runs UNTRUSTED document parsers with egress=none, ro-rootfs, all-caps-dropped, non-root. The worker only needs to: read stdin, decompress a zip (CPU+mem), parse XML via lxml/libxml2 (and openpyxl/pypdf), write one JSON object to stdout, and exit. It performs NO network I/O (network is also disabled at the runtime layer — this profile is defence-in-depth, NOT the sole egress control). This is INTENTIONALLY tighter than the gateway profile (yashigani-gateway.json): NO socket/bind/connect/listen/accept (the worker never networks), NO ptrace/process_vm_* (no introspection), NO mount/pivot_root/chroot/unshare/setns (no namespace fiddling), NO bpf/perf_event_open, and clone is FLAG-FILTERED to reject every CLONE_NEW* bit (clone3 forced to ENOSYS so glibc falls back to the filtered clone).
_su_review_decisions_2026-06-09: Su review CLOSED, all four Captain asks answered with runtime evidence on Docker 29.1.3 + Podman 3.4.4 (containment harness 6/6 both). (1) ALLOWLIST MINIMAL: verified — 122->121 syscalls after dropping execveat (real docx/xlsx/pdf parse jobs pass with execve kept + execveat removed; see harness). (2) ZERO NETWORK: confirmed — socket/connect/bind/listen/accept/sendto/recvfrom/sendmsg/recvmsg and all socket-family syscalls are ABSENT (programmatically asserted); egress harness CASE 5 green both runtimes. (3) CLONE FLAG-FILTER: ADDED — clone is now allowed ONLY when arg0 (flags) carries no CLONE_NEW* bit (SCMP_CMP_MASKED_EQ against the CLONE_NEW* mask 0x7e020000), the docker-default strategy; an adversary calling clone(CLONE_NEWUSER) gets EPERM (proven: rc=-1 errno=1), while CPython thread creation still works (proven: threads start, all parse jobs exit 0). clone3 cannot be flag-filtered (its args live in a struct, not a register) so it is forced to ENOSYS (errnoRet 38) — glibc then falls back to the filtered clone. This is belt-and-suspenders ON TOP OF the existing absence of unshare/setns + no-new-privileges + cap_drop ALL. (4) execve/execveat: execve is REQUIRED (removing it breaks the OCI 'exec /usr/local/bin/python' entrypoint — proven: 'operation not permitted'); execveat is REMOVED (all parser paths pass without it). DEFAULT ACTION: kept SCMP_ACT_ERRNO (not KILL) — matches docker-default + the gateway profile, lets glibc probe-and-fallback (clone3->clone, statx->fstat), and a denied syscall surfaces as a clean Python exception -> non-zero worker exit -> fail-closed BLOCK; KILL adds no containment (syscall is blocked either way) but risks breaking benign CPython/lxml feature-probes on minor-version bumps.

syscalls._group: file IO on stdin/stdout/tmpfs only (no network fds — see review)
threading + scheduling (CPython threads; clone is flag-filtered below)
process startup (execve REQUIRED for the OCI entrypoint exec; execveat dropped — no parser path needs it, Su 2026-06-09)

clone — thread creation ONLY: allow when arg0 (flags) carries no CLONE_NEW* bit. Mask 0x7e020000 = CLONE_NEWNS|NEWCGROUP|NEWUTS|NEWIPC|NEWUSER|NEWPID|NEWNET. SCMP_CMP_MASKED_EQ (flags & mask) == 0 (this is the docker-default strategy). CPython thread creation (CLONE_VM|FS|FILES|SIGHAND|THREAD|...) carries none of these bits and is permitted; an attempt to create a new namespace (e.g. clone(CLONE_NEWUSER)) is denied with EPERM.
clone3 — cannot flag-filter (args are in a struct, not a register). Force ENOSYS (38) so glibc falls back to the flag-filtered clone above. Matches the docker-default profile.
